transcript: In early May 2023, the Chippewa County Sheriff's office received reports about a series of threatening messages directed toward the courthouse in Chippewa Falls, Wisconsin.
The individual behind the threats, 39-year-old Harley Alcala, was scheduled to appear in court for a custody hearing involving his daughter.
Just one day prior to that hearing, Harley posted disturbing messages on Facebook.
In them, he demanded to see his daughter and made violent threats against the courthouse and the employees.
Among the posts were statements such as, I'm showing up to get my daughter at 1:30.
1:30 tomorrow or die.
Ever see a courthouse shot up?
End their F and lives if I don't walk out with my daughter.
Several other posts echoed similar threats of violence, raising serious concern.
In addition, one of the rants specifically targeted the judge overseeing his case.
The sheriff's office quickly contacted the district attorney's office to report the threats.
Given the timing, the nature of the posts, and Harley's upcoming court appearance, the threats were deemed credible.
The courthouse was placed on lockdown, and it was determined there was enough probable cause to proceed with Harley's arrest.
The next day, investigators quickly obtained Harley's phone number through a separate department in the jurisdiction where he lived.
By pinging the device, they were able to pinpoint his movements and determine he was heading south on US Highway 53 toward Chippewa County.
Several surrounding law enforcement agencies were notified, and a joint operation was launched to locate Harley and carry out a high-risk traffic stop.
Baron County deputies subsequently located Harley's vehicle and began following him, alerting Chippewa County, who soon joined in and initiated the traffic stop.
